description: Effect-link graph: 'change A => must review/change B'. Seeded with architecture invariants; grows as code lands (see protocols/effect-link.md).

to.ref: @tessera/storage:adapters/* (sqlite, sqlite-vec, filesystem, in-proc; later postgres, pgvector, s3, bullmq)

links.rationale: A storage port is implemented by every adapter and validated by one shared conformance suite; changing the interface breaks all adapters and the suite, and a semantic change needs an ADR.
The KG/effect-link model feeds graph retrieval, get_effects, and the dashboard; the harness effects schema mirrors the product model, so changing one should be reflected in the others.
The public API/MCP contract is the source for OpenAPI + the generated SDK + the dashboard; any contract change must regenerate and update all three (API is additive/versioned).

from.ref: docs/design/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md design tokens (semantic CSS variables / theme)

links.rationale: Components consume design tokens only; changing a token or the theme contract affects every component, the chart palette, and both themes, so token changes must be reviewed across the UI.
CI runs the verification gates; the workflow steps and the verification protocol table must mirror gates.json. Adding/removing/renaming a gate requires updating the CI workflow and the protocol.

to.ref: every @tessera/* package and app (all depend on @tessera/core)

links.rationale: @tessera/core is the dependency-free base every package imports; changing/removing an exported member (Id, errors, Result, config types, EventBus) can break any consumer. Change additively and review dependents.

to.ref: @tessera/storage adapters (sqlite-relational, filesystem-blob, in-process-queue; later postgres, s3, bullmq)

links.rationale: Each storage port is implemented by every adapter and validated by a shared conformance suite; changing a port interface breaks all adapters and their suites (concrete instance of E-001 for @tessera/storage).
